<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p xtask -- action-reference` from the action
     contracts (duhem_actions::catalog()). Do not edit by hand; run the command
     to regenerate. Drift-checked in CI. -->

# Action reference

Version-exact ground truth for authoring a Verification Definition — every
built-in action's `with:` fields, `outputs`, and a worked example. Generated from
the action contracts, so it always matches what `duhem validate` / `duhem run`
accept. `duhem describe <uses>` prints the same for one action; `duhem actions`
lists the catalog.

Bind an output with `outputs: { <name>: <field> }`, then read it in an
assertion as `$steps.<id>.outputs.<name>`. Assert over **scalar** outputs
(`status`, `body_text`, `satisfied`, `exit_code`, …); helpers like
`$runtime.contains(...)` cover membership.

## `ui/*`

### `ui/navigate`

Navigate the browser to a URL.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `url` | yes | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** (none)

```yaml
- uses: ui/navigate
  with: { url: https://example.com }
```

### `ui/click`

Click an element (locator shorthand fields, or a `locator` object).

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `role` | no | — |
| `label` | no | — |
| `testid` | no | — |
| `placeholder` | no | — |
| `css` | no | — |
| `name` | no | — |
| `text` | no | — |
| `scope` | no | — |
| `locator` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** (none)

```yaml
- uses: ui/click
  with: { role: button, name: "Save" }
```

### `ui/type`

Type text into an element.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `locator` | yes | — |
| `text` | yes | — |
| `clear` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** (none)

```yaml
- uses: ui/type
  with: { locator: { css: "#email" }, text: "a@b.com" }
```

### `ui/select`

Select an option in a <select> by value, label, or index.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `locator` | yes | — |
| `by` | yes | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** (none)

```yaml
- uses: ui/select
  with: { locator: { css: "#role" }, by: { label: "Admin" } }
```

### `ui/assert-element`

Assert an element reaches an existence/visibility state within a deadline.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `locator` | yes | — |
| `expected` | yes | `exists` \| `not_exists` \| `visible` \| `hidden` |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `satisfied`, `count`

```yaml
- uses: ui/assert-element
  with: { locator: { role: heading, name: "Welcome" }, expected: visible }
```

### `ui/assert-url`

Assert the page URL equals a string or matches a regex (exactly one of equals/matches).

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `equals` | no | — |
| `matches` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `satisfied`, `actual`

```yaml
- uses: ui/assert-url
  with: { equals: https://example.com/ }
```

### `ui/assert-state`

Assert an app state (e.g. signed in/out, or a marker) via `state:`.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `state` | yes | — |
| `marker` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `satisfied`

```yaml
- uses: ui/assert-state
  with: { state: signed_in }
```

## `api/*`

### `api/call`

Make an HTTP request and observe the response.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `method` | yes | — |
| `url` | yes | — |
| `headers` | no | — |
| `query` | no | — |
| `body` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `status`, `body`, `body_text`

```yaml
- uses: api/call
  with: { method: GET, url: https://example.com }
# then assert directly: $steps.<id>.outputs.status == 200 (no outputs: block)
```

### `api/observe`

Observe a network request the page made (matched by method + URL pattern).

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `method` | yes | — |
| `url_pattern` | yes | — |
| `after` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** (none)

```yaml
- uses: api/observe
  with: { method: POST, url_pattern: "**/api/login" }
```

### `api/poll`

Poll an HTTP endpoint until a condition holds or the deadline elapses.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `method` | yes | — |
| `url` | yes | — |
| `headers` | no | — |
| `body` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |
| `interval` | no | — |
| `until` | yes | — |

**outputs:** `satisfied`, `status`, `body`, `body_text`

```yaml
- uses: api/poll
  with: { method: GET, url: $inputs.job_url, until: "$response.body.state == 'done'" }
```

### `api/stream`

Consume a streaming (SSE) endpoint, collecting events until a condition.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `method` | yes | — |
| `url` | yes | — |
| `headers` | no | — |
| `body` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |
| `until_event` | no | — |
| `max_events` | no | — |

**outputs:** `status`, `events`, `event_count`, `last_event`, `stopped_reason`

```yaml
- uses: api/stream
  with: { method: GET, url: $inputs.events_url, until_event: done }
  outputs: { count: event_count }
```

## `db/*`

### `db/query`

Run a read query and observe the returned rows.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `connection` | yes | — |
| `sql` | yes | — |
| `params` | no | — |
| `find` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `rows`, `row_count`

```yaml
- uses: db/query
  with: { connection: $inputs.db, sql: "select id from users" }
  outputs: { n: row_count }
```

### `db/observe`

Poll a query until a condition holds or the deadline elapses.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `connection` | yes | — |
| `sql` | yes | — |
| `params` | no | — |
| `find` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |
| `interval` | no | — |
| `until` | no | — |

**outputs:** `satisfied`, `rows`, `row_count`

```yaml
- uses: db/observe
  with: { connection: $inputs.db, sql: "select status from jobs where id=1", until: "$rows[0].status == 'done'" }
  outputs: { satisfied: satisfied }
```

### `db/seed`

Run a write statement to seed or mutate data.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `connection` | yes | — |
| `sql` | yes | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `rows_affected`

```yaml
- uses: db/seed
  with: { connection: $inputs.db, sql: "insert into users(id) values (1)" }
```

## `cli/*`

### `cli/invoke`

Invoke a local command and observe its exit code, stdout, and stderr.

| `with:` field | required | values |
|---|:---:|---|
| `command` | yes | — |
| `cwd` | no | — |
| `env` | no | — |
| `stdin` | no | — |
| `within` | no | — |

**outputs:** `exit_code`, `stdout`, `stderr`

```yaml
- uses: cli/invoke
  with: { command: ["echo", "hi"] }
  outputs: { code: exit_code }
```
